title: Some customer phone numbers are wrong in TaskFlow
body: We noticed that some of our customer contact records have incorrect phone numbers. The numbers look truncated or mangled. Our support team caught it because they tried calling a few customers and got wrong numbers. We have about 2,000 customer records and I'd estimate maybe 50-100 have wrong numbers. This is a serious data integrity issue for us.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for flagging this — data integrity issues like this are definitely worth getting to the bottom of.

To help me understand the bigger picture: can you walk me through how these customer phone numbers typically get into TaskFlow? For example, are they entered manually by your team, imported from a spreadsheet or another system, or synced automatically from somewhere? And do you have a sense of when the numbers were still correct — was there a point recently where you know they were fine, or is it possible this has been building up over time?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: The phone numbers come from our CRM — we use Salesforce. There's a nightly batch import that syncs customer data into TaskFlow automatically. I'm pretty sure the numbers were fine until maybe 2 weeks ago? That's roughly when our support team started noticing the issue. It seems like it's been getting worse over time, like more numbers are getting messed up with each passing day. I don't think anyone on our team is manually editing these records.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful — the nightly Salesforce sync and the ~2 weeks ago timeline give us a lot to work with.

I'm curious about what the mangled numbers actually look like. Could you grab a couple of examples — say, what the number looks like in Salesforce versus what ended up in TaskFlow? For instance, are digits being chopped off the beginning or end, are there extra characters appearing, or is the formatting just different? The specific pattern of how they're wrong will tell us a lot about where things are breaking down.

Also, do you recall whether anything changed around that two-week mark — a TaskFlow update, a change to the Salesforce sync configuration, or maybe a change in how phone numbers are formatted on the Salesforce side?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
Let me check a couple examples... OK so one customer has +353 1 234 5678 in Salesforce (they're in Ireland) but in TaskFlow it shows as +35 1 234 5678 — the country code got cut short. Another one is from Antigua, their number starts with +1268 in Salesforce but TaskFlow only has +12 and then the rest of the number. But I checked a few US numbers (+1) and UK numbers (+44) and those all look fine.

As for what changed around that time — I'm not 100% sure but I think there was some kind of update to how TaskFlow handles phone numbers? One of our devs mentioned something about adding international number support a couple weeks back. I don't know the details though. Nothing changed on the Salesforce side as far as I know.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Phone numbers with 3+ digit country codes are truncated during Salesforce batch import","problem":"The nightly Salesforce-to-TaskFlow batch import is truncating international phone numbers whose country codes are longer than 2 digits. For example, Ireland's +353 becomes +35 and Antigua's +1268 becomes +12. Numbers with 1-2 digit country codes (US +1, UK +44) are unaffected. Approximately 50-100 of ~2,000 customer records are currently affected, and the count is growing with each nightly sync.","root_cause_hypothesis":"A recent change to TaskFlow's phone number handling (described as 'adding international number support', deployed ~2 weeks ago) almost certainly introduced a country code parsing or validation step that assumes country codes are at most 2 digits. This truncates valid 3-4 digit country codes (e.g., +353 Ireland, +1268 Antigua) during the import/normalization pipeline. The ITU E.164 standard allows country codes of 1-3 digits, and some combined codes like +1268 may be parsed as a 4-digit prefix depending on implementation.","reproduction_steps":["Ensure a Salesforce contact record exists with a phone number using a 3+ digit country code (e.g., +353 1 234 5678 for Ireland)","Wait for or manually trigger the nightly batch import from Salesforce to TaskFlow","View the imported contact record in TaskFlow","Observe that the country code has been truncated to 2 digits (e.g., +35 1 234 5678)"],"environment":"TaskFlow with Salesforce integration via nightly batch import. Issue began ~2 weeks ago, coinciding with a phone number handling update.","severity":"high","impact":"Support team cannot reach customers with 3+ digit country codes (many international regions including Ireland, India +91x, Caribbean nations, etc.). Data corruption is progressive — each nightly sync overwrites more records with truncated numbers. Affects an estimated 50-100 records currently but will grow as new records with long country codes are synced.","recommended_fix":"1. Identify the recent commit/PR that added 'international number support' to the phone number parsing or import pipeline. 2. Find the logic that extracts or validates country codes and fix the truncation (likely a substring, field-length, or regex issue capping at 2 characters). 3. Verify the fix handles country codes of 1, 2, and 3 digits correctly per E.164. 4. Re-run the Salesforce import (or a targeted re-sync of affected records) to restore correct phone numbers from the authoritative Salesforce data. 5. Consider adding a validation check that flags when an imported number differs from the source to catch similar issues earlier.","proposed_test_case":"Unit test for the phone number parsing/normalization function with inputs covering 1-digit (+1), 2-digit (+44), 3-digit (+353), and combined-prefix (+1268) country codes. Assert that all digits are preserved. Integration test: import a batch containing numbers from US, UK, Ireland, India, and a Caribbean nation, then verify all numbers match the source exactly.","information_gaps":["Exact commit or PR that introduced the phone number handling change","Whether truncated data is stored in the database or only mangled at display/export time (likely stored, since support team encountered wrong numbers when calling)","Whether the import process logs errors or silently truncates"]}
